entities.description: bookrag is a system whose performance is being evaluated across different query types
figure 7 is a visual representation that breaks down the performance of bookrag
single hop is a type of query used to evaluate the performance of bookrag
multihop is a type of query that presents a greater challenge compared to single hop queries
global aggregation is a type of query used to evaluate the performance of bookrag
the agent based planning strategy is a method used to handle different query types separately
qa performance refers to the quality of answers generated  which is analyzed under different query types
query types are the categories of questions used to evaluate the system s performance
disjoint pieces of evidence are the fragmented information sources that make reasoning difficult
retrieving is the process of finding information  identified as a challenge in the text
reasoning is the cognitive process of drawing conclusions  identified as a challenge in the text

relations.description: figure 7 displays the performance breakdown of bookrag
bookrag s performance is evaluated against single hop queries
bookrag s performance is evaluated against multihop queries  which present a greater challenge
bookrag s performance is evaluated against global aggregation queries
the agent based planning strategy is validated by its ability to handle multihop queries
the agent based planning strategy handles single hop queries separately
the agent based planning strategy handles global aggregation queries separately
qa performance is measured under different query types
qa performance is the metric used to evaluate bookrag s capabilities
multihop queries are challenging because they require retrieving and reasoning over disjoint pieces of evidence
retrieving is the action performed on disjoint pieces of evidence
reasoning is the action performed on disjoint pieces of evidence
